author: Sangharakshita
title: Contents of Fifteen Points for Buddhist Parents - Questions and Answers
content:
Questions and Answers on Fifteen Points for Buddhist Parents
23rd April 1994 at the London Buddhist Arts Centre
Contents


1 What to tell children of Buddhist parents about celebrations such as Christmas
2 A special puja for children? When can children start learning meditation?
Difficulties with Christian religious education in schools
3 Looking after one's friend's children in the Mov ement
4 Is a father/male important for a developing boy?
5 Doubts about paternity TV and computer games
6 'Materialism'
7 Single parents living together in communities? Can parents attain stream entry?
8 Teaching children to meditate 'Coming of age' ceremonies?
9 Trying to preserve a relationship 'for the sake of the children'
10 Can parenthood be a path that leads to insight?
An Anagarika and a Brahmachari
11 Extended families Do fathers and mothers play different roles in brin ging up children?
13 Feeling marginalised as a parent in the FWBO
14 Should children be regarded as little adults?
15 Equal parental responsibility? If so, from when ?
16 Doing things wholeheartedly
17 Are parents over-protective of their children?
18 Breaking a law because one thinks it is unjust?
19 Buddhist schools? Unplanned pregnancies
